The board Of public works Of the
city of Spokane will receive bids in
duplicate, until March II, 1904, at the
hour OF 2 o'clock p. m, for the i'm
provement Of First avenue from the
east line Of Madison street to the
east line of Walnut street by paving.
guttering, curbing, according to the
plans and specifications on file in the
Office Of the city engineer, and re-
ferred. to In Ordinance No. ALAS.
which ordinance and said map, plans
and specifications shall govern said
are hereby referred for further par
ticulars. All bids must be made in
duplicate on printed forms to be fur
nishsd by the board, sealed. and one
copy left with the clerk of the board,
the other with the mayor.


There must be written upon the
outside Of the envelope the words.
Proposal for the paving Of First
avenue" together with the name Of
the bidder. and the copy OF the bid
sent to the board must be accom-
panied with a certified check for not
less than 10 per cent of the amount Of
the bid and made out to the order Of
the president of the board of public


works, and no bid shall be considered
unless accompanied by said check.


The successful bidder shall be re-


quired to enter into a contract and

ed by said Ordinance No. A1448. the


laws of the state Of Washington, the

upon the subject within five days
after he has been notified that the
contract has been awarded to him.
Special attention Is called to Ordi-


nance No. A1o31. governing contracts
for public work. Where any material
used Is Of the kind that is produced
here the same shall be Of home pro-
ductton when prices and quality are
equal to foreign production.


ject any and all bids. Successful
